creator: asomers@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Ivan Rozhuk from comment #8)
What is utimens?  FreeBSD has no such thing.  Do you mean utimensat?  utimensat is a syscall.  The kernel can't call it.  All the kernel does it send upcalls like FUSE_SETATTR to the fuse server, which in this case is sshfs.  If anything is calling utimensat, it's sftp-server, which is what sshfs invokes on the ssh server.  The platform-specific value of UTIME_OMIT doesn't matter, because sshfs is compiled separately on each platform and any way UTIME_OMIT should never cross the wire over ssh.  Instead, the sftp protocol presumably has some other way to convey that meaning.

> Linux community too big to report and fix this issue years ago.

What do you mean?  Are you suggesting that you're unable to report issues to a large community, or that because it's a large community, surely this issue must've been fixed on Linux so you aren't going to check?  I'm having trouble following your English.

You really should try this on Linux with atime=on.  I think that by default Linux sets atime=off, which is probably why you didn't notice the problem there.
